title: Portfolio Management Techniques: How do portfolio managers typically differentiate between asset allocation and...
summary: Distinguish asset allocation across classes from security selection within classes.
prompt: How do portfolio managers typically differentiate between asset allocation and security selection?

options.text: Portfolio managers differentiate between asset allocation and security

solution: Portfolio managers differentiate between asset allocation and security selection by defining asset allocation as the process of deciding how to distribute investments across various asset classes (e.g., stocks, bonds, cash) while security selection focuses on choosing specific securities within those asset classes. This distinction allows for a structured approach to building and managing a portfolio.
